front: What is the difference between Flink savepoints and checkpoints?
back: Checkpoints are automatic, periodic snapshots created by Flink for fault tolerance — they're managed by Flink, may be deleted after newer checkpoints succeed, and are optimized for speed. Savepoints are manually triggered, user-managed snapshots that persist indefinitely until explicitly deleted. Savepoints are designed for planned operations: upgrading Flink versions, changing job parallelism, modifying operator logic, or A/B testing. Technically, savepoints use the same snapshot format as checkpoints but include additional metadata (operator UIDs) that enable restoring after code changes. Best practice: assign stable operator UIDs (uid('my-operator')) to all operators — without them, savepoints break on topology changes because Flink can't map old state to new operators. Savepoints are your 'git checkpoint' for production streaming jobs.
